curriculum.title: HTML Made Easy
curriculum.description: A step-by-step HTML curriculum designed for kids and absolute beginners. Learn slowly, clearly, and confidently.

lessons.title: What is a Webpage?
lessons.body: A webpage is a page you see on the internet. Webpages can show text, pictures, and links. Many webpages together make a website.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! Welcome to your first lesson. Today, you will learn what a webpage is. You see webpages every time you use the internet. Don't worry if this is new — we'll learn it slowly and have fun together.

questions.question: A webpage is something you can see in a browser.
questions.explanation: Correct! Webpages are what we see when we use the internet.

lessons.title: What is HTML?
lessons.body: HTML is a language used to build webpages. It tells the browser what to show on the screen.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello again! In this lesson, you will learn what HTML is and why we use it. HTML helps us create webpages. It's okay if the name sounds big — the idea is very simple.

questions.question: What does HTML help us make?
questions.explanation: HTML is used to create webpages.

lessons.title: What is an HTML Tag?
lessons.body: An HTML tag is a label. It tells the browser what something is. Tags use angle brackets like < and >.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! Today you will learn about something very important called an HTML tag. Tags are like name labels that tell the browser what things are. We'll look at a simple example.

questions.question: Tags tell the browser what something is.

lessons.title: Opening and Closing Tags
lessons.body: Most HTML tags come in pairs. One tag opens, and one tag closes. The closing tag has a slash / in it.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! In this lesson, you will learn how HTML tags open and close. This helps the browser know where something starts and where it ends.

questions.question: Which one is a closing tag?

lessons.title: Paragraph Tags
lessons.body: The p tag is used to write text on a webpage. It creates a paragraph.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! Today you will learn how to write sentences on a webpage using paragraph tags. This is how we show text on the screen.

code_example.description:
Here we use the paragraph tag again. The

tag starts the text, and

ends it. Everything inside becomes a sentence on the webpage. This is how we write words for people to read.

questions.question: Write a paragraph that says 'Hello HTML'
questions.explanation: Great job! You used the paragraph tag correctly.

code_example.explanation:
You opened the paragraph with

, wrote your text, and closed it with

. That's exactly how paragraphs work!

lessons.title: Headings
lessons.body: Headings are titles. HTML has different heading sizes from h1 to h6.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! In this lesson, you will learn about headings. Headings are titles that make text bigger and easier to see.

questions.question: h1 is bigger than h2.
questions.explanation: Correct! h1 is the biggest heading.

lessons.title: HTML Page Structure
lessons.body: A real HTML page has a structure. It has html, head, and body tags.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! Now it's time to put everything together. In this lesson, you will learn the basic structure of a real HTML page.

questions.question: Which part shows on the webpage?
questions.explanation: The body is what people see.

lessons.title: Links and Images
lessons.body: Links let us visit other pages. Images show pictures on our webpage.
lessons.avatar_script: Hello! In this final lesson, you will learn how to add links and images to your webpage.

questions.question: Images can be shown using HTML.
questions.explanation: Yes! HTML can show images on a webpage.
